description_long: This is a large, single-edged, curved blade resembling a machete or kukri, with a pronounced belly and a sharp, pointed tip. The blade is metallic, darkened with a weathered or forged texture, and features a continuous curve from the handle to the tip. The handle is straight, blocky, and appears to be made of wood, with a slightly squared-off end and a visible grain pattern. There is no visible guard between the blade and handle, giving it a utilitarian appearance. The blade's surface is matte with some subtle patterning, and the overall construction suggests it is designed for heavy-duty cutting or chopping tasks. No decorative elements or sheath are present.
description: A large, curved, single-edged blade with a dark, weathered metal finish and a straight wooden handle. The design is utilitarian, lacking ornamentation or a guard.
description_view_0: Side profile showing the full length of the curved blade and the straight wooden handle.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the thickness of the blade and the rectangular cross-section of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side profile displaying the blade's curvature and the handle's squared-off end.
description_view_3: Blade edge view emphasizing the curve and the transition from handle to blade.
attribute_blade_type: curved, single-edged, broad belly, pointed tip
attribute_handle_design: straight, blocky, wooden, squared-off end, visible grain
